name: Jucar Canyons Trail from Cortes de Pallas
name_original: Los cañones del Júcar — Circular desde Cortes de Pallás
description: This difficult 36.9-mile road cycling route through the Jucar canyons from Cortes de Pallas offers dramatic scenery and 4719 feet of gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cortes De Pallás traverse a rugged and steep landscape, characterized by large slopes and deep ravines. The region features dramatic natural formations such as the Júcar Canyon, with its vertical walls, and is surrounded by steep mountains like Pico Martés and La Muela de Cortés. These routes offer varied elevation changes and challenging terrain, often passing by the serene Cortes de Pallás Reservoir. Best road cycling routes around Cortes De Pallás The most popular road cycling route is Jucar Canyons Trail from Cortes de Pallas, a 36.9 miles (59.3 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route navigates the impressive Júcar Canyon, offering significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Ayora Hut – La Pileta Viewpoint loop from Cortes de Pallás, a difficult 55.4 miles (89.1 km) path. This route features extensive climbs through mountainous terrain, leading to panoramic viewpoints. Local road cyclists also love the Cofrentes Castle – Cofrentes Water Wheel loop from Venta de Gaeta, a 44.9 miles (72.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Cortes De Pallás is defined by deep ravines, steep mountains, and the Júcar River, offering a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents. The network primarily caters to experienced cyclists seeking demanding routes. The routes in Cortes De Pallás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 351 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cortes De Pallás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cortes de Pallás?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Cortes de Pallás, offering diverse experiences for cyclists. The region is particularly known for its challenging terrain.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Road cycling in Cortes de Pallás is characterized by rugged and steep landscapes, featuring large slopes and deep ravines. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, dramatic natural formations like the Júcar Canyon, and mountainous areas, making for a challenging and rewarding ride.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Cortes de Pallás?
A: The road cycling routes in Cortes de Pallás are predominantly difficult, catering to experienced cyclists seeking challenging climbs and descents. There are no routes classified as easy, and only one is considered moderate, suggesting the area is best suited for those comfortable with demanding terrain.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers breathtaking scenic viewpoints. You can expect expansive views of the Júcar Canyon and the village from the Tierra Colorá viewpoint, or impressive vistas of the Cortes de Pallás Reservoir from the ascent to Castillo de Chirel. Many routes traverse mountainous terrain leading to panoramic spots like La Pileta Viewpoint, featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Ayora Hut – La Pileta Viewpoint loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cortes de Pallás?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the dramatic natural scenery of the Júcar Canyon, and the varied elevation changes that make for an engaging ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Cortes de Pallás offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40320222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Millares – La Pileta Viewpoint loop from Charcas del Ral&lt;/a&gt; covers over 105 kilometers, providing an extensive ride through varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might encounter on a road cycling tour?
A: You'll encounter dramatic natural features like the Júcar Canyon with its vertical walls, the serene Cortes de Pallás Reservoir, and steep mountains such as Pico Martés. Historical landmarks like the Cofrentes Castle are also part of routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33725646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle – Cofrentes Water Wheel loop from Venta de Gaeta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Cortes de Pallás are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Ayora Hut – La Pileta Viewpoint loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33725646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle – Cofrentes Water Wheel loop from Venta de Gaeta&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Cortes de Pallás?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations vary, the region's climate generally allows for cycling outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn typically offer more moderate temperatures, ideal for tackling the challenging climbs and enjoying the scenery.

Q: Are there any caves or waterfalls near the road cycling routes for off-bike exploration?
A: Yes, the area boasts natural wonders like Cueva Hermosa, a grotto with stalactites and stalagmites, and the Fuente Corbinet waterfall with its turquoise pool. You can find more information about these and other geological formations in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3821930/caves-around-cortes-de-pallas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves around Cortes De Pallás guide&lt;/a&gt;, which includes highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4069862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Araña Cave and La Rebolla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the road cycling trails?
A: The routes often pass through picturesque villages, which may offer opportunities for refreshments. While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, planning your stops in advance, especially in smaller villages, is advisable for longer rides.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the road cycling routes?
A: The Cortes de Pallás reservoir is a hub for water activities, including swimming. Additionally, the Arroyo de Cortes features waterfalls and crystalline pools, offering potential spots for a refreshing dip off the bike. Always check local regulations and safety before swimming in natural waters.
tips: This route is not well known or traveled and that makes it perfect for riding a bike. The route has very nice views of the Muela de Cortes and the Júcar river ravine until it reaches the town of Millares. A beautiful, quiet and picturesque tour through the Valencian mountains!
A very picturesque stretch of road with very nice views of the Júcar valley and the Muela de Cortes. It is very little traveled which makes it ideal to enjoy a colorful tour with the "skinny".

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route from Cortes de Pallas is approximately 59.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. Road cycling in the Júcar canyons involves significant elevation changes, with approximately 1,438 meters of ascent. You should be prepared for considerable climbing and have good to very good fitness.

faqs.question: Where does the road cycling route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular tour starting from Cortes de Pallas. While specific parking details aren't provided, Cortes de Pallas is the natural starting point, and you'll likely find suitable parking within the village or near the main access roads.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling in the Júcar canyons?
faqs.answer: The terrain features winding, low-traffic country roads that traverse spectacular gorges carved by the Júcar River. You'll experience dramatic limestone cliffs, mountainous sections, and impressive views of the canyons and reservoirs. The roads are generally well-developed, but it's always wise to check for suitability for road bikes, as some regional routes might include unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What are the notable features or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary feature is the stunning natural beauty of the Júcar canyons. Along the way, you'll encounter a Beautiful mountain road and enjoy the Descent from La Muela to Dos Aguas, offering breathtaking views. The Cortes de Pallas reservoir and various natural viewpoints like "Tierra Colorá" also provide impressive vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Júcar canyons offer a pleasant cycling experience for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for challenging rides like this, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the road for safety reasons. If you plan to visit the area with a dog, it's best to look for specific hiking trails where dogs are permitted, usually on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Reserva Nacional de Caza Muela de Cortes?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Reserva Nacional de Caza Muela de Cortes. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the reserve. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding wildlife and protected areas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, pack essentials such as plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A detailed map or GPS device is also highly recommended due to the winding nature of the roads.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: Cortes de Pallas, as your starting and ending point, will likely have options for cafes or places to refuel. Along the route itself, especially in the more remote canyon sections, services may be limited, so it's advisable to carry sufficient provisions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route overlap with other official trails. Approximately 15% of the route covers sections of the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós trail, and about 6% intersects with the Ruta del Oro. A small portion also touches the [CIMA VA06] Muela de Cortes trail.

waypoints.name: Descent from La Muela to Dos Aguas
